questions.text: The mean of 5 numbers is 18. If one number is removed and the mean becomes 16, the removed number is:
questions.explanation: Total of 5 numbers = 5 × 18 = 90. Total of remaining 4 = 4 × 16 = 64. Removed number = 90 − 64 = 26.

questions.text: The mode of the data: 3, 5, 7, 5, 3, 5, 8, 3, 5 is:
questions.explanation: Count: 3 appears 3 times, 5 appears 4 times, 7 once, 8 once. Mode = 5 (most frequent).

questions.text: The median of the data: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 is:
questions.explanation: Data is already arranged. 7 values → median = 4th value = 5.

questions.text: In a bar graph, the height of each bar represents:
questions.explanation: In a bar graph, the height (or length) of each bar represents the frequency (or value) of the corresponding category.

questions.text: The probability of an impossible event is:
questions.explanation: An impossible event can never occur. Its probability = 0.

questions.text: A fair coin is tossed. The probability of getting heads is:
questions.explanation: A fair coin has 2 equally likely outcomes (H, T). P(Head) = 1/2.

questions.text: In a pie chart, the total angle at the centre is:
questions.explanation: A pie chart is a circle. Total angle at the centre of any circle = 360°.

questions.text: The mean of the first 10 natural numbers is:
questions.explanation: Sum of first 10 natural numbers = 10×11/2 = 55. Mean = 55/10 = 5.5.

questions.text: A die is rolled. Probability of getting a number greater than 4 is:
questions.explanation: Numbers greater than 4 on a die: {5, 6} = 2 outcomes. P = 2/6 = 1/3.

questions.text: The range of the data: 15, 8, 22, 4, 19, 11 is:
questions.explanation: Range = Maximum − Minimum = 22 − 4 = 18.

questions.text: In a histogram, the class width is represented by:
questions.explanation: In a histogram, the width of each bar represents the class interval (class width). The height represents frequency.

questions.text: If P(E) = 0.65, then P(not E) is:
The median of an even number of observations is:

options.b: The average of the two middle values

questions.explanation: For even number of observations, median = average of the two middle values after arranging in order.

questions.text: A bag has 5 red, 3 blue and 2 green balls. Probability of picking a blue ball is:
questions.explanation: Total balls = 10. Blue balls = 3. P(blue) = 3/10.

questions.text: A double bar graph is used to:

options.a: Show data for one category only
options.b: Compare two sets of data for the same categories

questions.explanation: A double bar graph has two bars for each category — it is used to compare two sets of data side by side.

questions.text: The angle in a pie chart for a category with 25% share is:
A card is drawn from a well-shuffled deck of 52 cards. Probability of getting a king is:
questions.explanation: There are 4 kings in 52 cards. P(king) = 4/52 = 1/13. Note: 4/52 = 1/13 so both c and d are the same value — c is the simplified form.

questions.text: The probability of a certain event is:
questions.explanation: A certain event will definitely occur. Its probability = 1.

questions.text: In a grouped frequency table with class interval 20-30, the class mark (midpoint) is:
questions.explanation: Class mark = (lower limit + upper limit)/2 = (20+30)/2 = 25.

questions.text: The mean of 6, 8, 10, 12, 14 is:
The mean of 8 numbers is 35. Two numbers 40 and 30 are added. What is the new mean?
In a pie chart, sector for 'Sports' has an angle of 72°. If total students = 300, how many students play sports?
The median of: 3, 8, 5, 1, 9, 6, 4, 7, 2 is:
questions.explanation: Arrange in order: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. 9 values → middle (5th) = 5.

questions.text: Two dice are rolled. The probability of getting a sum of 7 is:
A survey of 200 students shows 40% prefer cricket, 35% prefer football and 25% prefer badminton. The angle for cricket in a pie chart is:
The following marks are given: 45, 50, 55, 60, 65, 70, 75, 80. The median is:
questions.explanation: 8 values (even). Middle two = 4th and 5th = 60 and 65. Median = (60+65)/2 = 62.5.

questions.text: A bag has 6 red and 4 blue balls. Two balls are drawn one after another without replacement. Probability that both are red is:
The mean of a data set is 12. If each value is increased by 3, the new mean is:
questions.explanation: Adding a constant k to each value increases the mean by k. New mean = 12 + 3 = 15.

questions.text: From a pack of 52 cards, a card is drawn at random. Probability it is a red face card is:
questions.explanation: Red face cards = King, Queen, Jack of Hearts + King, Queen, Jack of Diamonds = 6 cards. P = 6/52 = 3/26.

questions.text: The mean of 5 observations is 15. If one observation of value 20 is replaced by 10, the new mean is:
questions.explanation: Original total = 75. New total = 75 − 20 + 10 = 65. New mean = 65/5 = 13.

questions.text: The mean of 10 numbers is 20. The mean of 6 of them is 15. What is the mean of the remaining 4?
questions.explanation: Total of 10 = 200. Total of 6 = 90. Sum of remaining 4 = 200 − 90 = 110. Mean = 110/4 = 27.5.

questions.text: Three coins are tossed simultaneously. Probability of getting exactly 2 heads is:
questions.explanation: Sample space = {HHH, HHT, HTH, THH, HTT, THT, TTH, TTT} = 8 outcomes. Exactly 2 heads: HHT, HTH, THH = 3. P = 3/8.

questions.text: A histogram shows the following frequency distribution: 0-10: 5, 10-20: 8, 20-30: 12, 30-40: 10, 40-50: 5. The class with the highest frequency is 20-30. What is the modal class and the approximate mode?
questions.explanation: The class with the highest frequency (12) is 20-30 → it is the modal class. The mode is approximately the midpoint of the modal class = (20+30)/2 = 25.

questions.text: In a pie chart showing monthly expenses, the angle for food is 135°, rent is 90°, education is 45° and savings is the remainder. If monthly income is ₹24000, the amount saved per month is:
A class of 40 students has mean marks 65. The top 10 students have mean marks 90. What is the mean of the remaining 30 students?
questions.explanation: Total marks = 40 x 65 = 2600. Top 10 total = 10 x 90 = 900. Remaining 30 total = 2600 - 900 = 1700. Mean = 1700/30 = 56.67.

questions.text: A number is selected from 1 to 30. Probability that it is divisible by both 3 and 5 is:
questions.explanation: Divisible by both 3 and 5 = divisible by 15. Numbers: 15, 30 = 2 numbers. P = 2/30 = 1/15.

questions.text: Find the mean of: 12, 18, 24, 30, 36. Enter the answer.

questions.explanation: Arranged: 2, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 13, 15. 9 values → 5th value = 8.

questions.text: A card is drawn from 52 cards. What is the probability of drawing an ace? (Express as a fraction in simplest form, e.g. 1/13)
questions.explanation: 4 aces in 52 cards. P = 4/52 = 1/13.

questions.text: The angles in a pie chart for a monthly budget are: Food 120°, Rent 90°, Clothing 60°, Savings ?°. Find the angle for savings.
The mean of 5 numbers is 28. Four of the numbers are 25, 30, 22, 35. Find the fifth number.
Two dice are thrown. Find the probability of getting a doublet (same number on both dice). Express as a fraction.
The following data shows marks of 6 students: 48, 56, 70, 42, 63, 71. Find the range.
questions.explanation: Range = Maximum − Minimum = 71 − 42 = 29.

questions.text: In a pie chart, the angle for a particular item is 45°. The total is 720 units. How many units does this item represent?
A bag has 4 white, 5 red and 6 blue balls. A ball is drawn at random. Find the probability that it is NOT red. Express as a fraction in simplest form.
questions.explanation: Total = 15. Not red = 4+6 = 10. P(not red) = 10/15 = 2/3.

questions.text: The mean of 20 observations is 45. Later it is found that one observation 54 was wrongly recorded as 45. Find the correct mean.
questions.explanation: Original total = 20 × 45 = 900. Correct total = 900 − 45 + 54 = 909. Correct mean = 909/20 = 45.45.

cases.case_text: A school conducted a survey of 360 students about their favourite subject. The results: Maths 90, Science 72, English 54, Social Science 72, Hindi 72. The teacher wants to display this data in a pie chart.

subparts.text: What is the angle for Maths in the pie chart?
What angle does Science occupy in the pie chart?
The angle for English in the pie chart is (in degrees):

cases.case_text: In a game, a die is rolled and a coin is tossed simultaneously. A player wins if they get an even number on the die AND a head on the coin.

subparts.text: What is the total number of outcomes in the sample space?
subparts.explanation: Die has 6 outcomes, coin has 2. Total = 6 × 2 = 12 outcomes.

subparts.text: What is the probability that the player wins (even number AND head)?
The probability that the player does NOT win is:
